approvalPolicy: Leave first-send approval template decisions null unless a founder/operator explicitly approves, edits, or rejects a row. This artifact does not authorize live sends.
globalCta: Reply with the one workflow lane your team would want captured and hosted first. If the labeled sample is useful, inspect it as proof shape only.
globalProofBoundary: Demand-sourced rows do not have target-specific captures, site-world packages, package access, or hosted reviews yet.
The labeled sample link is representative proof shape only, not a customer result, not recipient-specific proof, and not deployment evidence.
Recipient-backed evidence proves the address source only; it does not prove buyer intent, permission, customer status, or approval to make unsupported claims.
No live send, reply, hosted-review start, qualified call, paid spend, sender durability, Notion mutation, Gmail polling, SendGrid, Nitrosend, or dispatch authorization is approved by this artifact.
No pricing, legal, privacy, rights, permission, readiness, deployment, provider execution, package access, or guaranteed support commitment is approved by this artifact.

rows.recommendedLane: dock, trailer unload, pallet, or case-handling movement
rows.proposedSubject: Boston Dynamics: which dock workflow should Blueprint capture first?
rows.proposedBody:
Hi,

I am building Blueprint, a capture-backed way for robot teams to turn real facility workflows into site-specific world-model packages and hosted review sessions.

For Boston Dynamics, I saw the public Stretch warehouse case-handling and unloading signal. I do not want to guess the wrong facility. If Blueprint captured one structured-facility workflow for your team first, which lane would be worth hosting for review?

The short menu is: dock, trailer unload, forklift, pallet, or case movement; fulfillment aisle, tote flow, induction, picking, or replenishment; retail aisle or customer-facing service route; or humanoid/AMR route review inside a structured facility.

My first guess for Boston Dynamics would be a trailer unload, dock, pallet, or case-handling lane.

Reply with the one workflow lane your team would want captured and hosted first. If the labeled sample is useful, inspect it as proof shape only: /sample-evaluation?workflow=warehouse-amr

Blueprint does not have a Boston Dynamics-specific capture or hosted review yet. This is a capture ask only, not a claim that your site is already captured or available.

Nijel
rows.draftAngle: Narrow the broad capture ask to Stretch-relevant dock, trailer, pallet, and case-handling lane selection.
rows.cta: Reply with the dock, trailer, pallet, or case-handling workflow worth capturing first.
rows.proofSource: Draft opportunity brief at ops/paperclip/playbooks/exact-site-hosted-review-first-target-brief.md. Labeled sample may be inspected only as proof shape.
rows.objectionPlan: If they ask whether Blueprint has their facility captured, say no: this row is asking which workflow to capture first.
